bio: I'm Ka Vatana, a Year 1 Software Engineering student from Cambodia. My path started with mathematics, STEM activities, public speaking, volunteering, and digital marketing before moving deeper into software engineering and product building.

Right now my strongest proof project is Chomkar.com, an agritech platform focused on market access for farmers. It demonstrates my project evolution from KasekorX, an AI voice-to-marketplace idea for farmers, into Chomkar.com and won Top 2 at Turing Hackathon Cycle 10 under the theme Market Access for Farmers.

I study Software Engineering at BELTEI International University, study Year 1 English at IFL, build Chnai Lab as a student-led digital project team, and keep improving this portfolio as the public home for my projects and learning journey.

My 2026 track also includes a July 2026 - October 2026 web system and digital project support contract with Angkor Byte, where I am learning professional workflow, communication, implementation support, and Cambodia-Japan technology collaboration.

I want the public version of my identity to stay honest: I am not presenting myself as a senior developer. I am a student builder learning seriously through real projects, real community experience, and steady practice.
role: Year 1 Software Engineering Student · Product Builder · Cambodia
aiNote: I use AI tools to help research, plan, code, and review — while learning to understand and verify the result myself.
currentFocus: Chomkar.com: market access for farmers and agritech product validation
Chnai Lab: student-led team building, digital products, and automation
BELTEI International University: software engineering fundamentals
IFL: Year 1 English for academic, technical, and professional communication
Angkor Byte: July 2026 - October 2026 web system and digital project support
kavatana.me: a professional but honest archive of my growth
